agents.description: Registered agent identities and their permission bindings.

budget.description: Default resource budget for teams and flows.

cognitive_core.description: Path to cognitive core directory (personas, heuristics, directives).

credentials.description: Credential label → backend source mappings. Only credentials listed here are accessible to agents.

discover.description: Domains to query for AID upstream discovery at startup.

models.description: Named model definitions (embedding, classification, chat, generate).

permissions.description: Named permission sets referenced by agents.

pii_patterns.description: Custom PII patterns applied globally to all safety pipelines. Categories defined here are treated as PII for IFC labeling.

registry.description: Whitelisted MCP servers to advertise in the registry. These appear in the /v0.1/servers endpoint alongside navra's own modules and connected upstream servers.

server.description: Server transport, identity, and container settings.

statistical.description: Statistical guardrail configuration for anomaly detection.

temporal_contracts.description: Temporal behavioral contracts for trajectory-level policy enforcement.

triggers.description: Event-driven triggers that start flows automatically.

upstream.description: Upstream MCP servers to proxy through the gateway.

capability_token.description: Enable capability token issuance for this agent.

name.description: Unique agent identifier used in logging and auth.

permissions.description: Name of the permission set to apply (must match a key in `[permissions]`).

pubkey.description: Path to Ed25519 public key file for capability token auth.

signing_key.description: Path to an Ed25519 private key for commit signing. When set, git_commit signs commits with this key using Git's SSH signing support (gpg.format=ssh).

token_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the agent's bearer token.

token_ttl.description: Override token TTL for this agent (seconds).

AgenticConfig.description:
Operator-defined agentic capabilities for a model.

Configured in `[models.<name>.agentic]` and merged into the model card at startup.

```toml [models.granite-code.agentic] strengths = ["code generation", "fast inference"] weaknesses = ["limited reasoning", "small context"] recommended_tasks = ["code review", "simple analysis"] avoid_tasks = ["multi-step planning"] tool_use = "basic" cost_tier = "free" speed_tier = "fast" ```

avoid_tasks.description: Task types this model should not be used for.

max_agents.description: Maximum concurrent agents this model can serve.

recommended_tasks.description: Task types this model is recommended for.

strengths.description: What this model is good at (free-text tags for routing).

grant_ttl_secs.description: Time-to-live for cached approval grants in seconds (default: 300 = 5 min). After approval, the agent has this long to retry the operation.

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for human approval responses (default: 300).

BudgetConfig.description:
Default resource budget for teams and flows.

Defaults are generous for autonomous operation — agents get enough iterations, time, and depth to converge on thorough results. The operator tunes down via config for cost control.

```toml [budget] max_agents = 50       # total across all teams/subflows max_depth = 5         # escalation nesting depth timeout_secs = 1800   # 30 minutes per flow tree max_iterations = 200  # per agent ReAct iterations max_parallel = 4      # concurrent agents (GPU bound) checkpoint = true     # enable SQLite checkpoint for crash recovery checkpoint_db = "~/.local/share/navra/checkpoints.db" ```

checkpoint.description: Enable SQLite-backed checkpointing for DAG execution crash resilience.

checkpoint_db.description: Path to the checkpoint SQLite database.

head_ratio.description: Ratio of budget allocated to head in head_tail strategy.

max_agents.description: Maximum total agents across all teams/subflows (default: 50).

max_parallel.description: Maximum tasks running simultaneously (GPU throttling). 0 means no limit.

max_tool_output_tokens.description: Maximum tool output tokens before truncation (0 = disabled).

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds per flow tree (default: 3600).

CredentialMapping.description:
A credential source mapping from config.

Maps a label (e.g., "github.pat") to a backend source (keyring path, environment variable, etc.).

DiscoveryConfig.description:
AID (Agent Identity & Discovery) configuration.

Populates the `/.well-known/agent` JSON endpoint per the AID spec. See: https://aid.agentcommunity.org/docs/specification

description.description: Human-readable description (max 60 bytes per AID spec).

mdns.description: Enable mDNS/DNS-SD advertising and browsing on the local network.

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for AID HTTP lookups and mDNS browse (default: 10).

url.description: Externally-reachable URL of this server's MCP endpoint. Example: "https://tools.example.com/mcp"

DomainRuleConfig.description: A domain rule entry in config.

domain.description: Domain name (must be a valid navra Domain: filesystem, git, shell, etc.) Use "*" as wildcard default for unlisted domains.

operations.description: Allowed operations for this domain. Empty list = deny all.

ExecutionMode.description:
How a model executes: loaded in-process (ONNX) or served via a runtime backend (llama.cpp, vLLM). Decouples model *purpose* (embedding, classification, chat) from *how it runs*.

Default: `from_task()` preserves current behavior — embedding and classification run in-process, chat/generate run served. Operators can override in config: `execution_mode = "served"` on an embedding model to serve it via vLLM instead of loading it in-process.

oneOf.description: Load model directly into the navra process (ONNX Runtime).
Spawn a dedicated inference server (llama.cpp, vLLM).

db.description: SQLite database path for the file index.

default_root.description: Default root path for file_tree when no path argument is given. Overrides the top-level `cognitive_core` setting for docs routing.

enabled.description: Enable the file module. Default: true.
Enable the git module. Default: true.
Enable the GitHub module. Default: true.
Enable the GitLab module. Default: true.

GrpcModuleConfig.description: Configuration for a single gRPC module.

command.description: Command to spawn the module process (first element is binary, rest are arguments). Empty if the module is externally managed.

max_restarts.description: Maximum number of restarts before giving up (default: 3).

name.description: Module name (used in logging and tool registration).

restart_on_failure.description: Whether to restart the module on failure (default: true).

socket.description: Unix socket path for the module endpoint.

IdentityConfig.description:
Root identity configuration.

Controls where navra stores its Ed25519 root keypair and how capability tokens are issued.

key_path.description: Path to Ed25519 seed file. If omitted, the OS keyring is used.

nonce_cache_ttl_secs.description: Nonce cache TTL in seconds for replay tracking (default: 7200 = 2 hours).

token_ttl.description: Default capability token TTL in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour).

KvCacheType.description:
KV cache quantization type.

For llama-server: controls `--cache-type-k` and `--cache-type-v`. For vLLM: maps to `--kv-cache-dtype` (fp8). Lower precision reduces VRAM usage at the cost of quality.

audit_retention_days.description: Auto-delete audit log entries older than N days. Default: 365 days (1 year).

pii_filter.description: PII filter profile for memory ingestion and audit logs. Uses the same profiles as safety: "standard", "secrets-only", "none". Default: "standard".

pii_retention_days.description: Stricter TTL for entries flagged as containing PII. Default: 30 days.

retention_days.description: Auto-delete knowledge entries older than N days. Default: None (keep forever).

ModelConfig.description:
Configuration for a model.

Models can be loaded from local files (ONNX) or pulled from registries and served via a runtime backend.

agentic.description: Operator-defined agentic capabilities for model selection.

api_key.description: API key for authenticated model servers.

base_url.description: Base URL for remote model servers (e.g., OGX, custom OpenAI-compatible). Overrides the default URL for the chosen runtime.

cache_type.description: KV cache quantization type for runtime-served models.

context_size.description: Context window size for runtime-served models (default: 4096).

device.description: Execution device for ONNX models: "cpu", "cuda", "openvino", "openvino:AUTO", "openvino:NPU", "openvino:GPU". Defaults to "cpu" if not specified.

execution_mode.description: Execution mode: "in_process" (ONNX in gateway process) or "served" (spawn inference server). Auto-derived from `task` if not set: embedding/classification → in_process, chat/generate → served. Override to serve an embedding model via vLLM or load a small chat model in-process.

format.description: Model format: "gguf", "safetensors", "awq", "gptq". Auto-detected from the model path if not specified.

locality.description: Data locality: "local" (default) or "remote". Remote models require content filtering before sending.

model_name.description: Model name for the OpenAI-compatible API. Defaults to the config key.

model_path.description: Path to a local model file (ONNX). Used directly when no `source` is set.

parallel.description: Number of parallel request slots for runtime (default: 1).

runtime.description: Runtime backend: "auto", "podman", "direct", or "none" (default). Used for chat/generate tasks served via navra-model-runtime.

source.description: Hub source URI (e.g. `ollama://granite3.3:8b`, `hf://org/repo`). When set, the model is pulled and cached via navra-model-hub.

tokenizer_path.description: Path to the HuggingFace tokenizer.json file.

ModelTierConfig.description: A single tier in the routing configuration.

max_tokens.description: Maximum output tokens for requests routed to this tier.

model.description: Model identifier to use for this tier.

patterns.description: Tool name glob patterns that route to this tier.

memory.description: Persistent memory module with PII-aware retention.

registry.description: MCP server registry and discovery module.

api_key_value.description: API key value (or env var reference).

basic_password.description: Basic auth password (or env var reference).

bearer.description: Bearer token (or env var reference like "${JIRA_TOKEN}").

default_permissions.description: Default permission set when no label matches.

http_timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds for JWKS fetches (default: 5).

label_mapping.description: Maps OpenShell label expressions to navra permission set names. Example: "role=worker" -> "restricted"

oneOf.description: Verify JWT-SVID against SPIRE trust bundle.

audience.description: Expected audience claim (if set, audience validation is enabled).

trust_bundle_path.description: Path to SPIRE agent trust bundle PEM.

oneOf.description: Verify JWT against OIDC provider JWKS endpoint.
Trust Unix socket peer credentials. Returns InvalidToken to let the chain continue to other authenticators.
Verify JWT signed by OpenShell gateway's Ed25519 key.

audience.description: Expected audience claim (if set, audience validation is enabled).

public_key_path.description: Path to the gateway's Ed25519 public key PEM.

allow.description: Glob patterns of allowed file paths (e.g., "~/Code/**").

approve.description: Operations requiring human approval before execution.

can_delegate.description: Whether agents with this permission set can delegate capabilities.

compliance.description: Compliance framework tags for this permission set. Informational — logged at startup for audit trail. Example: ["SOC2-CC6.1", "EU-AI-Act-Art-14", "HIPAA-164.312"]

credentials.description: Credential labels this permission set can access.

default_tool_policy.description: Default policy for tools not matched by any rule: "allow", "deny", "approve"

deny.description: Glob patterns of denied file paths (e.g., "**/.env").

domain_rules.description:
Semantic domain rules: which operations are allowed per domain. Evaluated before glob-based tool_rules as an additional gate. If absent, domain-based enforcement is skipped (backward compat).

Example: ```toml domain_rules = [ { domain = "filesystem", operations = ["read", "write"] }, { domain = "shell", operations = [] }, { domain = "*", operations = ["read"] }, ] ```

rate_limit.description: Rate limit: maximum tool calls per window (e.g., "60/60" = 60 calls per 60 seconds).

ring.description: Privilege ring (0 = most privileged, 3 = most restricted). When set, deny rules and approval requirements cascade from lower-numbered rings, and operations are intersected.

safety_patterns.description: Custom regex patterns for content safety filtering.

safety_thresholds.description:
Per-category confidence thresholds for multi-label safety models.

Used when `safety = "multi-label"`. Each key is a category name (e.g., "harm", "jailbreak", "pii") and the value is the minimum confidence score (0.0-1.0) to trigger filtering for that category.

Example: ```toml [permissions.dev.safety_thresholds] harm = 0.7 jailbreak = 0.9 pii = 0.5 refusal = 0.8 ```

tainted_write_policy.description: IFC policy for tainted writes: "allow", "approve", or "deny". When an agent reads external data (taint rises to Untrusted), this policy controls whether subsequent writes are permitted.

tool_class.description:
Override auto-classification for specific tools. Keys are tool names, values specify domain and operation.

Example: ```toml [permissions.readonly.tool_class] zip_files = { domain = "filesystem", operation = "write" } ```

tool_disclosure_exclude.description: Tool disclosure: glob patterns of tools to hide from tools/list.

tool_disclosure_include.description: Tool disclosure: glob patterns of tools to show in tools/list. Empty = show all tools. This is UI-level filtering only — agents can still call hidden tools if they know the name.

tool_rules.description: Per-tool permission rules (evaluated before handler invocation).

trusted_paths.description: File paths that should keep their Trusted integrity label even when accessed via external read tools. Supports glob patterns. Example: ["~/Code/myproject/**", "~/Documents/**"]

PiiPatternConfig.description:
A custom PII pattern for global content safety filtering.

Categories defined here are treated as PII for IFC labeling, unlike `SafetyPatternConfig` which only triggers redaction/blocking.

db.description: Database path. Defaults to the same directory as docs, separate file.

enabled.description: Enable the RAG module. Default: true.

query_cache_max_entries.description: Maximum number of cached query entries (default: 1000).

query_cache_ttl_secs.description: Query cache TTL in seconds (default: 300). Set to 0 to disable caching.

reranker_model_path.description: Path to the ONNX cross-encoder model for reranking (optional).

reranker_tokenizer_path.description: Path to the tokenizer.json for the cross-encoder model (optional).

RegistryEntry.description: A whitelisted MCP server for the registry.

registry_type.description: Registry type: "mcp", "http", "aws_agent_registry". - "mcp": queries an MCP registry endpoint (default) - "http": generic HTTP/JSON registry with configurable URL template - "aws_agent_registry": AWS Agent Registry (future)

results_path.description: JSON path to extract results from the HTTP response (default: root array). Example: "data.results" to extract from `{"data": {"results": [...]}}`

search_url.description: URL template for search queries (HTTP type only). Use `{query}` as placeholder for the search term. Example: "https://registry.example.com/api/search?q={query}"

cache_ttl_secs.description: Cache TTL for registry responses in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour).

enabled.description: Enable the registry module. Default: true.

RoutingConfig.description: Configuration for the routing hook, deserializable from TOML.

default_tier.description: Default tier when no rule matches.

tiers.description: Model tiers, ordered from smallest to largest.

SafetyPatternConfig.description: A custom regex pattern for safety filtering.

agent_signature_policy.description: Agent bundle signature policy: "enforce", "warn", or "skip". Controls whether `navra agent install` requires cosign signature verification. Default: "warn".

allow_direct_execution.description: Allow direct (unsandboxed) execution when no container runtime is available. Default: `false`.

config_watch.description: Watch the config file for changes and hot-reload.

config_watch_debounce_ms.description: Debounce interval in ms for config file watch events.

containerized.description: Use containerized agent execution. `true` = always, `false` = never, absent = auto-detect Podman.

discovery.description: AID discovery configuration. When set, navra serves `/.well-known/agent` for the AID fallback protocol.

identity.description: Root identity configuration for DID-based auth.

mcp_version.description: MCP protocol version: "2026-07-28" (stateless dispatch, default) or "2025-03-26" (legacy session-based, deprecated).

model_server_image.description: Container image for the shared model server.

openshell_auth.description: OpenShell identity federation config. When set, OpenShellAuthenticator is inserted into the auth chain.

openshell_gateway.description: OpenShell compute driver gRPC endpoint for agent sandboxing.

pii_model_path.description: Path to PII NER model directory (English). Default: ~/.local/share/navra/models/pii-ner/

pii_multilingual_model_path.description: Path to multilingual PII NER model directory. Default: ~/.local/share/navra/models/pii-ner-multilingual/

tcp.description: TCP listen address (e.g., "127.0.0.1:9315"). Used instead of socket when set.

SpeculativeModelConfig.description: Speculative decoding configuration for runtime-served models.

draft_min_p.description: Minimum probability threshold for draft acceptance (default: 0.0).

draft_model.description: Model name or path for the draft (fast) model.

draft_tokens.description: Number of tokens to draft per step (default: 5).

StatisticalGuardrailServerConfig.description:
Server-side configuration for the statistical guardrail hook.

```toml [statistical] enabled = true cosine_window = 50 cosine_z_threshold = 3.0 entropy_window = 20 entropy_min = 0.5 entropy_max = 4.0 block_on_anomaly = false ```

block_on_anomaly.description: Whether to block tool calls when anomalies are detected. Default: false (monitor/warn only).

cosine_window.description: Sliding window size for cosine drift detection. Default: 50.

cosine_z_threshold.description: Z-score threshold for cosine drift anomaly detection. Default: 3.0.

enabled.description: Whether statistical guardrails are enabled. Default: false.

entropy_window.description: Sliding window size for entropy monitoring. Default: 20.

transition_min_observations.description: Minimum observations before transition anomalies are flagged (default: 10).

transition_window.description: Sliding window size for tool-transition anomaly detection (default: 50).

TemporalContractConfig.description: A single temporal contract definition in server config.

applies_to.description: Permission sets this contract applies to (glob patterns, "*" = all).

name.description: Unique contract name for logging and diagnostics.

TemporalContractServerConfig.description:
Server-side configuration for temporal behavioral contracts.

```toml [temporal_contracts] enabled = true max_history_per_session = 200

[[temporal_contracts.contracts]] name = "read-before-write" description = "Must read a file before writing" predicate = { type = "requires", tool = "file_write", prerequisite = "file_read" } action = { type = "block", value = "Read the file first" } applies_to = ["*"] ```

enabled.description: Whether temporal contracts are enabled. Default: false.

max_history_per_session.description: Maximum action history entries per session. Default: 200.

ToolClassConfig.description: Classification override for a specific tool.

domain.description: Domain to assign (must be a valid navra Domain).

operation.description: Operation to assign (must be a valid navra Operation).

ToolRuleConfig.description: A per-tool permission rule in config.

TriggerConfig.description: Configuration for a single trigger, deserialized from TOML.

flow_name.description: Name of the flow to start when triggered

path.description: URL path suffix, e.g. "/hook/deploy" becomes POST /hook/deploy

secret.description: Optional HMAC-SHA256 secret for request verification

flow_name.description: Name of the flow to start on schedule

schedule.description: Cron expression: "minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week"

flow_name.description: Name of the flow to start when a matching file changes

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter events, e.g. "*.pdf"

auth.description: Authentication for the target API (used with openapi upstreams).

enabled.description: Enable or disable this upstream. Default: true (absent = enabled).

name.description: Unique name for this upstream server.

openapi.description: OpenAPI 3.x spec source (URL or file path). When set, transport/command/url are ignored — navra parses the spec and exposes operations as MCP tools directly.

tool_class.description:
Per-tool semantic classification overrides. Keys are tool names, values specify domain and operation. Takes precedence over auto-classification and tool_overrides.

Example: ```toml [upstream.tool_class] zip_files = { domain = "filesystem", operation = "write" } ```

tool_filter.description: Tool name filter — only expose operations matching these names. Matches against both the prefixed tool name and the raw operationId. Supports glob patterns (e.g., "get_*", "*_search").

tool_overrides.description: Per-tool operation overrides: "read", "write", or "deny". Overrides the auto-classification from MCP annotations and name heuristics. Tools set to "deny" are never registered.

enabled.description: Enable the vision module. Default: true.

model.description: Name of the vision model in [models.*] config.

asr_model.description: Name of the ASR model in [models.*] config.

enabled.description: Enable the voice module. Default: true.

silence_timeout_ms.description: Silence timeout in milliseconds before auto-stopping recording (default: 1500).

tts_model.description: Name of the TTS model in [models.*] config.
